scope: the assertion that 'Warfarin sodium 5mg PO daily, INR target 2.0-3.0' is the current anticoagulant regimen, AND the reversal protocol of FFP + Vitamin K
corrected_assertion: As of 2024-03-15, warfarin was DISCONTINUED. Patient was switched to Apixaban 5 mg PO BID (twice daily) for the same non-valvular AFib indication. Reason: persistent INR lability in 2023 (4 episodes of INR >3.5 despite dose adjustments) + new diagnosis of moderate CKD (eGFR 48) prompting transition to a direct oral anticoagulant with more predictable pharmacokinetics. Apixaban dose-adjusted to 2.5 mg BID would only apply if any TWO of: age ≥80, weight ≤60 kg, serum creatinine ≥1.5 — patient meets only the renal criterion (cr 1.42), so standard 5 mg BID is correct. THE REVERSAL AGENT IS DIFFERENT: andexanet alfa for life-threatening bleed (NOT FFP, NOT Vitamin K — vitamin K does nothing for factor Xa inhibitors). For non-life-threatening bleed: hold dose, supportive care, charcoal if ingestion <2 hr. 4F-PCC 50 units/kg as alternative if andexanet alfa unavailable.
operator_confirmed: 2024-03-15T14:22:00Z Dr. Marcus Chen, MD (Cardiology) — co-signed by Dr. Elena Vasquez, MD (PCP)
retention_policy: PERMANENT. Original warfarin note retained as historical record — patient may rotate back to warfarin if apixaban intolerance develops, in which case append a further amendment. The 2008 reversal protocol DOES NOT apply while apixaban is the active agent. Any clinician reading the original file MUST treat this amendment as authoritative.

scope: the broader implication that this patient should receive FFP/Vitamin K in any acute bleed scenario
corrected_assertion: DO NOT administer FFP or Vitamin K to this patient for anticoagulant reversal. Patient is on Apixaban (factor Xa inhibitor) as of 2024-03-15. Correct reversal hierarchy: (1) Andexanet alfa per institutional protocol (loading: 400mg IV bolus over 30 min, then 4 mg/min ×120 min for low-dose protocol; 800mg/8mg/min for high-dose), (2) 4F-PCC 50 units/kg IV if andexanet unavailable, (3) supportive care + charcoal if recent ingestion. Vitamin K has NO ROLE in factor Xa inhibitor reversal. Administering FFP wastes critical time and may not reverse the bleed.
retention_policy: PERMANENT while apixaban is active medication. Re-evaluate if patient rotates back to warfarin or transitions to another anticoagulant class.
